_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-3 Transport Destination and Diversion (Revised 1/27/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-3: Transport Destination and Diversion — MDHHS Approved 1/27/2023

sections.title: Transport Destination — General Principles
points: Transport to the most appropriate facility based on patient condition, distance, and facility capabilities
Patient preference for a specific hospital should be honored when clinically appropriate and the facility is capable of managing the patient's condition
Patient preference does NOT override medical necessity — transport to the closest appropriate facility when time-sensitive condition exists
MCA-defined destination protocols and bypass criteria take precedence over patient preference in time-sensitive emergencies

sections.title: Time-Sensitive Destination Protocols
points: STEMI: transport to PCI-capable facility per MCA STEMI destination protocol; notify cath lab en route
STROKE: transport to Stroke Center per MCA stroke destination protocol; use stroke scale results to guide facility selection (Primary vs. Comprehensive Stroke Center)
TRAUMA: transport to highest-level trauma center within reasonable transport time per MCA trauma destination criteria
CARDIAC ARREST: transport to nearest appropriate ED or as directed by MCA protocol
PEDIATRIC CRITICAL: transport to pediatric-capable facility per MCA protocol
OB EMERGENCY: transport per MCA OB/High-Risk Delivery Transport Protocol (Section 8-4)

sections.title: Diversion
points: Hospitals may request diversion status — EMS is not obligated to honor diversion for critical or time-sensitive patients
CRITICAL PATIENTS (cardiac arrest, STEMI with cath lab activation, major trauma, active delivery): do NOT divert — transport to closest appropriate facility regardless of diversion status
Diversion may be considered for stable, non-time-sensitive patients when MCA protocols permit
Contact Medical Control if uncertainty exists about diversion and patient condition
STEMI destination override: if the nearest PCI facility is on diversion, contact medical control and receiving facility — diversion for STEMI cath lab activation patients is not standard practice

sections.title: Interfacility Transport
points: Interfacility transports: destination is typically determined by the sending facility's order and the patient's condition and insurance
EMS may decline an interfacility transport that exceeds their scope of care — contact Medical Control
Refer to Section 8-15: Interfacility Patient Transfers Protocol for staffing and scope requirements

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document destination selection rationale in PCR
Document any diversion status and crew decision regarding diversion
Document patient or family preference if different from destination selected
Document medical control contact if applicable

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must define STEMI destination criteria including preferred PCI-capable facilities and transport thresholds
options: MCA-defined STEMI destination protocol in place
Contact Medical Control for STEMI destination

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must define stroke center destination criteria (Primary vs. Comprehensive) based on stroke scale score
options: MCA-defined stroke destination protocol in place
Contact Medical Control for stroke destination

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must define trauma destination criteria based on mechanism, injury, and facility capabilities
options: MCA-defined trauma destination protocol in place
Contact Medical Control for trauma destination

deterioration_flags: Patient deteriorates during transport — consider diverting to closer facility; contact Medical Control
Receiving facility on diversion for critical patient — do NOT divert; notify receiving facility; contact Medical Control if needed
scope_notes: Destination: most appropriate facility based on condition, distance, and capability
Patient preference honored when clinically appropriate
Time-sensitive override: STEMI, stroke, trauma, cardiac arrest, critical pediatric, OB emergency
Critical patients: do NOT honor diversion
Document destination rationale and any diversion decision
MCA must define STEMI, stroke, and trauma destination protocols
purpose: Govern transport destination selection and diversion decisions for prehospital EMS. Patient preference is considered but does not override medical necessity. Time-sensitive conditions (STEMI, stroke, trauma) require destination decisions based on facility capability, not proximity alone. Critical patients should not be diverted.
